evals.expected_output: Should display current git branch and working directory in single-line format: 'Branch: <branch> | Dir: <path>'

assertions.name: output_contains_branch
assertions.description: Output should contain 'Branch:' followed by a branch name

assertions.name: output_contains_dir
assertions.description: Output should contain '| Dir:' followed by a path

assertions.name: cross_platform_paths
assertions.description: Should handle both Unix (/path/to/dir) and Windows (C:\path\to\dir or /c/path/to/dir) path formats

evals.prompt: Show me the current working directory and git info
evals.expected_output: Should display current git branch and working directory in single-line format: 'Branch: <branch> | Dir: <path>'

assertions.name: output_contains_branch
assertions.description: Output should contain 'Branch:' followed by a branch name

assertions.name: output_contains_dir
assertions.description: Output should contain '| Dir:' followed by a path

evals.prompt: I want to commit my changes
evals.expected_output: Should show the status display (branch and directory) before proceeding with commit operation, using brief single-line format

assertions.name: shows_status_before_commit
assertions.description: Should show branch/dir info before proceeding with commit

assertions.name: proceeds_with_commit
assertions.description: Should proceed to help with commit operation

evals.expected_output: Should display branch and directory in brief format. Optionally include brief uncommitted changes summary if git status was run

evals.prompt: Where am I in the project?
evals.expected_output: Should display branch and directory information in single-line format

evals.prompt: Show me git status on macOS
evals.expected_output: Should work correctly on macOS with Unix-style paths

assertions.name: handles_macos_paths
assertions.description: Should display Unix-style paths on macOS

evals.expected_output: Should work on Windows with either native paths (C:\path) or Git Bash paths (/c/path)
